notes: Excellent dual theorem/computational candidate. General optimal static STT remains explicitly open in Sadeh-Kaplan-Zwick 2025. Paths and stars are baselines for exact optimization; open LP questions concern paths' LP/root-rounding behavior and almost-star/edge-diameter-3 classes.

Still one of the best OpenEvolve-first candidates, but the pre-2024 'prove anything nontrivial' gap is solved: Kuszmaul-Xi prove constant expected insertion time at load factor at least 0.089 for fixed-offset schemes. Remaining work is model-specific threshold/load-factor improvement.

Survives adversarial audit as one of the best Batch 002 evaluator candidates. Keep confidence at medium until noisy/uncertain-comparison follow-ups are separated from the exact imprecise-comparison model.

Survives adversarial audit as a good OpenEvolve-first candidate, but the blind prompt's O(sqrt n) baseline is stale and range alpha-majority should stay separate. Verify the exact modern linear-space upper/lower-bound frontier before top-shortlist promotion.

Explicit Dagstuhl open problem with unusually concrete small-instance oracles. Downgrade evaluator fit from 5 to 4 because randomized false rejection and deterministic exact collision detection are different tasks.

Standard two-pass pairing heap exact decrease-key complexity remains open, but this is a hard, well-known specialist problem. Best used for potential-function/counterexample mining, not a first theorem proof.
Good evaluator-first candidate. The global randomized ratio gap is old and narrow; finite-state/non-projective search is useful but may not generalize.

Only promote the pointer-model version with an exact working-set definition. Broad non-pointer formulations are ambiguous because recent Dijkstra universal-optimality work already gives heaps with working-set guarantees and O(1) amortized decrease-key-like Fibonacci bounds.

Promising niche theorem project. The explicit open gap is external-memory biased search trees with linear block space and weighted I/O guarantees; OpenEvolve fit is weak until a crisp finite evaluator exists.

Batch 003 audit keeps the explicit ITCS 2025 dynamic-stream min-cut gap, but downgrades OpenEvolve fit: finite sketch/collision searches are hypothesis generators, not evidence for the asymptotic communication lower bound.
Dagstuhl gives explicit questions, but this folder bundles LZ indexing and grammar/DAG length-sampling. Split before promotion; recover missing notation from PDF/talk notes.

Batch 003 audit keeps the explicit lower-bound/future-work lead, with PDF-verified main bounds O((n+C) log^3 n) space/construction and O(k log^3 n) query. Downgraded because implementation heuristics and finite oracle tests can mislead.
Explicit Tarjan/Dagstuhl proof-simplification problem, not a new inverse-Ackermann theorem. Best as blind proof/Lean formalization after extracting the exact Seidel-Sharir recurrence.

Batch 003 audit keeps only the exact n-cell implicit scan/range-reporting gap. Broad cache-oblivious scan claims are stale because non-implicit/PMA-style structures support range scans; modern implicit follow-ups must still be checked.

Downgraded. Arbitrary-initial-tree Splay preorder/traversal still appears unresolved in checked sources, but the area is saturated and nearby Greedy/offline-OPT results have narrowed the useful target. Promote only a sharply defined Splay-vs-Greedy/initial-tree subproblem.

Batch 003 audit keeps the explicit DISC 2025 directed roundtrip-routing problem, but raises saturation risk and downgrades evaluator fit; finite graph search mostly falsifies toy routing rules and does not certify compact-routing lower bounds.
Downgraded. The min-tree-cut subroutine is a plausible niche, but dynamic min-cut is saturated and recent 2025/2026 progress must be read before treating the subroutine gap as current.
Downgraded by adversarial audit. Interesting KDS context, but the candidate is too broad and line-level open status remains unverified; narrow to one 3D measure before promotion.

Downgraded. The 2007 open question is stale without reconciling later dynamic compressed self-index results and precise bit/compression models; small-instance evaluators would be misleading for the theorem-level space claim.

Downgraded. Incremental topological ordering near ~O(m^(4/3)) is already achieved randomized by Bhattacharya-Kulkarni; Batch 002 dynamic-stream min-cut is explicit but streaming/lower-bound context, not a promoted DS candidate.
Keep as context only. The classic external-memory DecreaseKey necessity question has a lower-bound answer; remaining work/I/O tradeoff questions need a separate precise candidate.

Batch 003 audit downgrades sharply: the residual is inferred, and the STOC 2025 abstract lower-bounds two-element cells under stronger progress assumptions, so the one-key-vs-two-key formulation may be wrong. Use first for model extraction/formalization.

Downgraded. Dagstuhl records an active question cluster, not a precise theorem target; the Batch 002 concurrent hashing lead is already partially resolved by 2025 work and needs a residual model-specific gap.
Batch 002 retroactive lower-bound strengthening is inferred, not explicitly sourced. Keep as background until a named problem/model/residual lower-bound gap is identified.
Batch 002 all-purpose hashing residual is too vague and recent-active for promotion. Extract one explicit model-specific tradeoff before scoring higher.

Downgraded. Naor-Teague's 2001 variable-size allocation question is stale after the FOCS 2023 strongly history-independent storage-allocation result; only model-specific residual tradeoffs may remain.

Downgraded by Batch 002 adversarial audit. The Straka source likely solves the stated O(n+m) space, worst-case O(log log m) target; keep only as background/formalization unless a stricter residual open problem is sourced.
